content: Identity cards serve as official identification for employees and students and are required for access to services, restricted areas, and verification of status and eligibility.
All faculty, staff, and students must wear their identity cards at all times while on the NIT Calicut campus and produce them when requested by authorized personnel.
NIT Calicut issues Student ID Cards, Regular Faculty and Staff ID Cards, Ad-hoc and Contract Staff ID Cards, Pensioner ID Cards, and Visitor ID Cards.
Identity cards shall contain name, registration or employee ID number, department, course details, and emergency contact information as per institute policy.
One hundred visitor ID cards shall be maintained by the Security Officer, issued to authorized visitors, collected after visits, and recorded in an issue register.
Students use blue tags, regular faculty and staff use red tags, ad-hoc and contract staff use brown tags, visitors use white tags, and pensioners use black tags.
The Librarian is responsible for printing and issuing ID cards through the Central Library ID Card Section.

Students require authorization from Assistant Registrar (Academic), regular employees from Assistant Registrar (Establishment), contract staff from Assistant Registrar (Estate and IRM), and loss-related reissue from the Registrar.
Existing employee ID cards shall be withdrawn and replaced with new cards upon surrender of old cards. Existing student cards shall continue with new colour-coded tags.
Newly admitted students receive ID cards after admission and registration. Newly joined staff must submit approved forms before card issuance.
Applications for duplicate ID cards must be submitted in the prescribed format and recommended by the Head of Department or Reporting Officer.
A fee of Rs.300 is charged for duplicate cards due to updates or damage. Lost or stolen cards require payment of Rs.1000 for the first replacement.
For repeated loss of ID cards, a fee of Rs.2000 and a copy of the FIR are required along with an undertaking regarding responsibility for misuse.
Replacement of an ID tag requires payment of Rs.100.
Students and employees must return their ID cards upon course completion, discontinuation, resignation, or retirement. Failure to do so may prevent issuance of No Dues certificates.

section: Authorities Authorized to Demand ID Cards
content: Director, Registrar, Security Staff, Faculty, Heads of Departments, Deans, and Group A Officers may require individuals to produce identity cards as specified in Annexure A.
Employee ID card applications require personal details, contact information, department information, emergency contact details, photograph, and signature.
Employees must certify that the information provided is correct and undertake to report any future changes in personal or official details.
